competencies: Coordinate and run a simplified start-up team project to a product demonstrator
Develop a microsystems-based product idea and build a prototype
Conduct market analysis and assess business-plan feasibility
Prepare a marketing pitch, project portfolio and presentation
description_en: Team project realizing a microsystems-based product idea as a start-up demonstrator with market analysis, prototype, pitch and portfolio.

competencies: Analyze application-specific working principles of micro systems
Evaluate design aspects and fabrication of MEMS devices
Independently research and present a chosen micro-systems topic
Discuss and critique design choices for specific micro-system examples
description_en: Seminar exploring real-world applications, design and fabrication of micro systems through student-led topic presentations and discussion.

competencies: Perform worst-case electromagnetic compatibility analyses of electronic systems
Select and quantitatively evaluate interference-suppression measures
Apply numerical electromagnetic field computation methods
Assess signal and power integrity in interconnect structures
description_en: Course on analyzing electromagnetic compatibility of electronic systems using field theory, numerical methods and signal/power integrity.

competencies: Derive and apply the working principles of micro-optical devices
Assess limitations of micro-optical imaging and beam-shaping elements
Explain fiber-optic and meta-optical device concepts
Apply geometric, wave and Fourier optics to micro-optics
description_en: Course on the working principles, design and limitations of common micro-optical devices for imaging, beam shaping and fiber optics.

competencies: Estimate mechanical response of MEMS components from first principles
Analyze solid-mechanics behavior of cantilever and spring systems
Model micro-fluidic flow and surface-tension effects
Select micro-actuator principles for MEMS solutions
description_en: Course on the mechanical engineering of MEMS, covering solid mechanics, micro-fluidics and actuator principles at the micro scale.

competencies: Plan additive and subtractive fabrication routes for micro systems
Select cleanroom and rapid-prototyping processes for MEMS
Choose suitable materials for micro-system fabrication
Apply structural, chemical and dynamic MEMS characterization techniques
description_en: Course on fabrication processes, materials and characterization techniques for micro-electromechanical systems and microelectronics.

competencies: Design, build and test sensor-based systems for given tasks
Acquire and process measurement data with microcontrollers
Select and evaluate sensors by working principle for application domains
Document and present a two-semester practical sensor project
description_en: Two-semester practical project designing, building and testing sensor systems with microcontroller-based data acquisition and evaluation.

competencies: Independently work on and solve a scientific research problem
Recognize scientific relationships within a research question
Document results in a scientific final report
Present and defend findings in a colloquium
description_en: Scientific research project in which students independently address a research question and document and defend results in a colloquium.

competencies: Apply analytical and numerical methods to analyze electrical networks
Simulate electromagnetic field problems with finite-difference methods
Map mechanical systems onto equivalent electrical networks
Use MATLAB, Octave, Python or SPICE for system simulation
description_en: Course on analytical and numerical methods for simulating electrical networks and electromagnetic field problems using computational tools.

competencies: Formulate and structure mechatronic design problems
Apply the V-model of systems development
Use hierarchical and component-based design procedures
Evaluate parameter sensitivity, robustness and system optimization
description_en: Advanced course on integrated mechatronic system design using the V-model, component-based methods, robustness and optimization.

competencies: Model and simulate multi-domain mechatronic systems
Couple mechanical, electrical, thermal and hydraulic components in one model
Use Matlab/Simulink and toolboxes for mechatronic simulation
Build vehicle models capturing drive, steering, braking and driving dynamics
description_en: Course on modeling and simulating multi-domain mechatronic systems, including vehicle models, with Matlab/Simulink tools.

competencies: Work on a guided scientific research task at a chair
Acquire subject expertise and recognize relationships
Develop solutions through independent scientific work
Present results in a final report and colloquium
description_en: Supervised research project in the Mechatronics master where students address a scientific task and present results in a colloquium.

competencies: Identify complex systems across engineering and biological domains
Mathematically describe complex systems and evaluate their complexity
Assess stability and fragility of complex systems
Apply numerical tools and control methods to complex systems
description_en: Introduction to modeling, analyzing and controlling complex systems, including their stability, fragility and numerical treatment.

competencies: Derive multiscale mathematical models of chemical and biochemical production systems
Model multiphase systems with interfacial transport phenomena
Apply advanced model-reduction techniques
Analyze and design complex continuum process systems
description_en: Advanced course on multiscale mathematical modeling, multiphase systems and model reduction for chemical and biochemical production systems.

competencies: Model systems combining continuous, discrete and event-driven dynamics
Describe hybrid dynamical systems with automata and Petri nets
Analyze stability, reachability and accessibility of hybrid systems
Design control and observation methods for hybrid systems
description_en: Introduction to modeling, analyzing and controlling hybrid dynamical systems that combine continuous, discrete and event-driven behavior.

competencies: Apply discrete-event system methods to real application domains
Model and analyze processes with advanced discrete-event concepts
Formulate and solve scheduling problems using genetic algorithms
Verify complex flexible production systems via discrete-event models
description_en: Course applying discrete-event system methods to scheduling and flexible production, including simulation, analysis and verification.

competencies: Design and implement controllers computationally in Matlab/Simulink
Auto-tune centralized and decentralized PI/PID controllers
Design robust multivariable controllers via H-infinity and mu-synthesis
Synthesize controllers using linear matrix inequalities and real-time implementation
description_en: Course on computer-aided control design and implementation in Matlab/Simulink, covering robust multivariable and real-time controllers.

competencies: Analyze and compute complex flows in process engineering apparatus
Treat compressible flows with friction, heat transfer and shock waves
Model laminar and turbulent boundary layers and turbulence
Analyze multiphase and non-Newtonian fluid flows
description_en: Advanced fluid mechanics course on complex flows including compressible, turbulent, multiphase and non-Newtonian flow phenomena.

competencies: Simulate complex flow processes using numerical flow simulation
Generate high-quality grids and choose appropriate discretization
Assess convergence, residuals and validate CFD results critically
Simulate turbulent, multiphase and non-Newtonian flows
description_en: Course on computational fluid dynamics covering grid generation, discretization, validation and simulation of turbulent and multiphase flows.

competencies: Quantitatively assess chemical reactions and their kinetics
Select suitable reactor types and design chemical reactors
Apply stoichiometry and chemical thermodynamics to reactor design
Compute mass transfer in heterogeneous catalysis and reactor heat balances
description_en: Course on quantitative assessment of chemical reactions, reactor selection and design including kinetics, mass transfer and heat balances.

competencies: Characterize adsorbents and quantify adsorption equilibria of mixtures
Design fixed-bed adsorbers for separation processes
Identify effective reaction rates of heterogeneous catalysts
Apply transient reactor operating modes and industrial chromatography concepts
description_en: Course on adsorption equilibria, fixed-bed adsorber design, heterogeneous catalysis kinetics and industrial chromatography processes.

competencies: Recognize population-dynamic mechanisms in disperse systems
Formulate and analyze population-balance models mathematically
Solve population balances with specialized numerical methods
Apply population-balance results to process design and operation
description_en: Course on formulating, analyzing and numerically solving population-balance models for disperse systems in particle and bioprocess engineering.

competencies: Model disperse systems using mass, energy and population balances
Analyze crystallization, polymerization and emulsion systems
Quantify kinetics and thermodynamics of dispersed-phase mechanisms
Model, optimize and design processes involving a dispersed phase
description_en: Course on modeling disperse systems via balance equations, covering crystallization, polymerization and emulsion process design.

competencies: Evaluate experimental production data with statistical methods
Perform regression and correlation analysis for linear and nonlinear models
Determine confidence intervals of model parameters
Set up factorial and central composite designs of experiments
description_en: Course on statistical evaluation of experimental data, regression analysis and design of experiments for first- and second-order models.

competencies: Apply molecular modeling tools across spatial and temporal scales
Use Monte-Carlo methods for equilibrium and dynamic simulations
Run molecular-dynamics simulations of diffusion and nucleation
Match process-engineering problems to adequate models and numerical methods
description_en: Course teaching molecular modeling and simulation as an engineering tool using Monte-Carlo, molecular-dynamics and quantum-mechanical methods.

competencies: Perform steady-state and dynamic process simulation in Aspen Plus/Dynamics
Model unit operations such as reactors, columns and heat exchangers
Configure recycle streams and overall process flowsheets
Evaluate alternative process variants for conceptual process design
description_en: Course on conceptual process design via steady-state and dynamic simulation of chemical processes using Aspen Plus and Aspen Dynamics.

competencies: Characterize disperse particle systems with statistical material properties
Analyze stochastic and dynamic particle conversion processes
Apply multiscale modeling and population balances to comminution and separation
Evaluate and design particle-processing process systems
description_en: Advanced course on multiscale modeling and simulation of stochastic, dynamic mechanical particle processes such as comminution and separation.

competencies: Plan and operate electrical power transmission and distribution grids
Derive measures to develop intelligent smart-grid structures
Assess grid security, dynamic security and black-out prevention
Integrate decentralized generators and model wind farms in grids
description_en: Advanced course on planning and operating electrical power grids, smart-grid development, grid security and integration of decentralized generation.

competencies: Assess effects of renewable electricity on the power supply system
Evaluate photovoltaic, wind and hydropower generation potentials
Analyze fuel cells and electrical energy storage options
Understand grid integration of decentralized renewable generators
description_en: Course on generating electricity from renewable sources and integrating photovoltaics, wind, fuel cells and storage into the energy system.

competencies: Explain the physics and conversion principles of wind energy
Design wind turbines using airfoil theory and performance curves
Compute partial-load behavior and power characteristics of turbines
Analyze grid integration and economic viability of wind farms
description_en: Course on wind energy conversion, wind turbine design and components, and the technical and economic aspects of grid integration.

competencies: Build physical models for power-grid calculations and simulations
Model switching devices and travelling-wave phenomena in grids
Develop expert systems for power-supply problem solving
Apply fuzzy techniques and neural networks in knowledge-based systems
description_en: Course on modeling electrical power grids and building knowledge-based expert systems using fuzzy techniques and neural networks.

competencies: Develop complex power-electronic circuits from basic topologies
Explain control and regulation methods for power-electronic circuits
Dimension circuits application-specifically and model them for control
Apply power electronics to feed renewable energy into the grid
description_en: Course on developing, dimensioning and controlling power-electronic circuits, with applications in feeding renewable energy into the grid.

competencies: Implement power-electronic circuits in complex power-supply systems
Design power supplies for renewable generation and electric vehicles
Dimension power-electronic systems application-specifically
Apply HVDC, storage and charging-system concepts in electromobility
description_en: Course on power-electronic systems for power supply, covering renewable generation, HVDC, storage and electric-vehicle charging.
